scoring.note: provisionalScore uses a deterministic heuristic for the intelligence half. The agent should replace heuristicIntelligenceScore with its own 6-dimension LLM evaluation, then compute finalScore = round(0.5*foundationalScore + 0.5*llmIntelligenceScore).

checks.label: AI bot access
checks.details: No AI bots blocked in robots.txt.

prioritizedFixes.title: Add structured data (JSON-LD) on key templates
prioritizedFixes.why: Agents rely on machine-readable entities and page intent signals.

prioritizedFixes.title: Standardize title and meta description quality
prioritizedFixes.why: Weak metadata reduces retrieval accuracy and snippet quality.

prioritizedFixes.title: Expand answer-first content on thin pages
prioritizedFixes.why: Agents need enough explicit facts and context to answer safely.

prioritizedFixes.title: Improve internal linking between key pages
prioritizedFixes.why: Better graph connectivity improves crawl and context propagation.

prioritizedFixes.title: Publish a valid llms.txt with heading, links, and policy info
prioritizedFixes.why: It gives AI systems a direct index of trusted URLs and constraints.

prioritizedFixes.title: Add an RSS or Atom feed
prioritizedFixes.why: RSS feeds help AI systems discover and track new content.

prioritizedFixes.title: Improve content structure with clear heading levels
prioritizedFixes.why: Well-structured H1 → H2 → H3 outlines help AI agents chunk, extract, and cite specific sections.

prioritizedFixes.title: Add recognized schema types to JSON-LD
prioritizedFixes.why: Named types like Organization, Product, Article help AI classify your content.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Date signals, update timestamps, and active maintenance indicators.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 0% pages have date signals; no RSS feed.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Does the content lead with direct answers to questions agents get asked?
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 13% pages have Q&A-style headings; 25% lead with definitions.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Density of statistics, data points, named sources, and in-text citations.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 75% pages include numeric evidence; 0% have author attribution.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Can an agent extract clean, self-contained passages to quote as citations?
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 63% pages have rich headings; 75% meet depth threshold.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Clean semantic HTML structure that survives extraction to markdown.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 75% clean heading hierarchy; 75% have single H1.

heuristicIntelligenceSignals.rationale: Substantive long-form content that covers topics with depth and breadth.
heuristicIntelligenceSignals.keyFinding: 75% pages meet depth threshold across 4 template types.

aiView.title: AI Meeting Notes—Perfect meeting memory | Notion
aiView.metaDescription: Capture every detail and deliver organized summaries—right where you plan, collaborate, and get things done.
headings: The only place where notes turn into progress.
Turn notes into follow-ups that land
Find answers when you want them
Security and privacy at the highest standards.
Stop splitting your attention in meetings.
The Business plan is designed for your best work.
aiView.textExcerpt: Pause Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing. Trusted by those at Tailored to every meeting. Every summary gives you exactly what you need to move work forward. 1:1s Team syncs Client calls Interviews Brainstorms AI Meeting - 1on1 AI Meeting - Events AI Meeting - ClientCalls AI Meeting - Interviews AI Meeting - Events Works everywhere you do. Capture conversations anywhere. Sync your calendar, and note-taking happens automatically. Works with any video tool It transcribes your system’s audio and mic. No setup, no bots. Even for face-to-face Capture notes in person on any device. Syncs with your calendar Notes get created, started, and shared with attendees for you. The only place where notes turn into progress. Act on meeting outcomes in Notion. Notion AI handles the follow-ups so you can focus on what’s next. Action items become your to-dos Your Notion Agent can help assign tasks with owners, priority levels and due dates, making sure they get done. Projects are updated automatically Get your Notion Agent to keep projects updated after meeting with new statuses, completed tasks, and revised timelines. Turn notes into follow-ups that land Create meeting recaps tailored to your audience with a simple prompt. Find answers when you want them Ask anything about past meetings and get context-rich answers. Security and privacy at the highest standards. Trusted by Fortune 500 companies worldwide with enterprise-grade protections built in. No training on your data We have contractual agreements with our AI subprocessors that prohibit the use of customer data to train their models. Configurable transcript retention Workspace owners can set auto-transcript deletion windows (Enterprise plan only) and decide whether to store audio locally. Built-in safeguards With audit logs, granular permissions, and SSO, your sensitive work stays secure. Consent disclosures Clear in-product reminders to obtain consent before using AI Meeting Notes. Secure en

aiView.title: Join Notion's Affiliate Program
aiView.metaDescription: Refer Notion to your friends and followers, and earn up to $50 per activated sign up plus 20% of year one revenue for each referral you drive. No limits to how much you can earn.
aiView.textExcerpt: How it works. Join the program Apply to become a Notion affiliate. Promote Notion to your audience Share your unique affiliate link through your channels. You’ll have access to our Affiliates Resource Center with promotional best practices, guides, videos, and more. Earn rewards Earn commissions up to $50 per activated sign up plus 20% of year one revenue for each referral you drive. It’s easy to generate multiple links and track your rewards through PartnerStack. I love sharing the tools I build inside of Notion. And Notion's affiliate program lets me get paid doing the things I already wanted to do. Thomas Frank Template Creator Who can apply? Bloggers and writers ✓ Entrepreneurs ✓ Educators ✓ Media networks (news, podcasts) ✓ Email newsletters ✓ Content creators & influencers ✓ Notion community leaders, ambassadors, and template creators ✓ AI influencers & content creators ✓ FAQs What is the Notion Affiliate Program? The affiliate program is a way for bloggers, writers, community owners, entrepreneurs, influencers, Notion creators, and others to spread the word about Notion and earn financial rewards. What qualifies as a successful conversion? Net new workspaces that upgrade to a paid Plus or Business plan within 180 days of clicking your affiliate link will be eligible for the affiliate commission. Commissions are paid on a last-click basis, so your link must be the last touch point before the user signed up. How can I create, track, and promote my affiliate link? You can create multiple links and see your stats in your dashboard by signing in to PartnerStack . Once you receive your personalized affiliate link, we recommend that you promote it on your blog, social profile, website, videos, etc (the more places the better). When you post or distribute content about Notion, your message must make it obvious that you have a financially compensated relationship with Notion. We need all promotions to be FTC compliant. A helpful guide can be found here . What if a use

aiView.title: Why we built Notion – About
aiView.metaDescription: A story of tools, the future of work, and how we want to blend your workflow into an all-in-one workspace.
headings: A story of tools and the future of work
aiView.textExcerpt: About Notion A story of tools and the future of work Hi there! If you’re reading this, you’re probably like me—spending most of your days in your office, in front of a computer. You probably have fifteen tabs open: one for email, one for Slack, one for Google Docs, and on, and on… But have you ever thought about where these “work tools” came from? Or why there are so many of them? To answer these questions, and to explain why we created Notion, we have to travel back in time. As people flooded to factories during the Industrial Revolution, many tools were invented to ease the management overload. Typewriters replaced illegible handwriting. File cabinets stored more information than any clerk could remember (and also won the gold medal at the 1893 Chicago World’s Fair!) Tools like these paved the way for offices as we know them today. Fast forward to the 1950s, when computers came into the workplace. At first, they were lifeless, room-sized monsters that operated on paper and punch cards. They were used solely for number-crunching. Then something magical happened in the 1970s! A generation of pioneers saw the computer as something far beyond a number-crunching machine. They dreamt a future where computers could amplify imagination (Alan Kay), augment intellect (Doug Engelbart), and expand our thoughts far beyond text on paper (Ted Nelson). A tool unlike anything we have seen before. Play Uh-oh! It looks like your ad blocker is preventing the video from playing. Please watch it on YouTube But things didn’t turn out that way. As the legend goes, when Steve Jobs “borrowed” the ideas behind personal computing from Xerox PARC, many subtle, but crucial, differences were lost. Hit play and listen to the man himself. And that’s where we are today. Google Docs made typewriters multiplayer. Dropbox brought file cabinets to the cloud. But conceptually, they evolved little beyond their Industrial Revolution ancestors. We duct-tape everything together with emails, copy/paste, and
